mission.title: qdrant: [rocksdb] Investigate compression scheme for RocksDB
mission.description: [rocksdb] Investigate compression scheme for RocksDB. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current qdrant deployment
steps.description:
Verify your qdrant version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant
helm list -n qdrant 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working qdrant installation.

steps.title: Review qdrant configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant qdrant configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant
kubectl get configmap -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=qdrant
```
A quick profiling session on a heavy write workload shows that Snappy can take up to 2% of CPU time.

The official doc says:

```
Use options.compression to specify the compression to use. 
By default it is Snappy.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [rocksdb] Investigate compression scheme for RocksDB
steps.description:
Introduction of text field index immutable state.

Immutable state has with guarantee that we cannot insert new values, but still can remove them. It is applied for non-appendable segments.

As optimization of RAM usage was achieved this avoiding of this field:
https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant/blob/master/lib/segment/src/index/field_index/full_text_index/inverted_index.rs#L63
This is a map from points into points documents. By side it's the same to the whole inverted index.

For immutable

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant
kubectl get events -n qdrant --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[rocksdb] Investigate compression scheme for RocksDB" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Introduction of text field index immutable state.

Immutable state has with guarantee that we cannot insert new values, but still can remove them. It is applied for non-appendable segments.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with qdrant installed or the issue environment reproducible.
